brief_notes: Strong immunology paper using photoconvertible models to track DC migration kinetics with temporal resolution. Strengths: innovative use of Kaede mice for timestamping, comprehensive multi-modal analysis (flow cytometry, scRNA-seq, multiplexed IHC), clear quantitative results with fold-changes and p-values, cross-species spatial validation (mouse and human), and therapeutic relevance via anti-PD-L1 data. Weaknesses: no figures, only subcutaneous tumor models tested, the scRNA-seq analysis lacks detail on cell numbers per dwell-time bin, no assessment of whether anti-PD-L1 effects are DC-intrinsic, and the 72h timepoint may not capture the full range of dwell-time effects. The dwell-time exhaustion concept is compelling but needs mechanistic dissection.
